title: TLS, SSE-S3, SSE-KMS, Bucket Keys, and Encryption Boundaries · Go Retrieval
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs tls, sse-s3, sse-kms, bucket keys, and encryption boundaries from browser, Go service, S3, CloudFront, security, billing, and stakeholder evidence, then challenges one retained misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for encryption and key-authorization contract after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate TLS in transit, S3 server-side encryption, KMS envelope encryption, and application client-side encryption by threat and feature tradeoff.
Choose SSE-S3, SSE-KMS, dual-layer KMS where required, or client encryption from audit, tenancy, performance, cost, and service integration needs.
Constrain IAM and KMS key policy, grants, service principal, encryption context, region, rotation, disable, deletion, and break-glass ownership.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Transport At Rest Client
steps.instruction:
Order the Transport At Rest Client S3, CloudFront, and Go storage-delivery evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Transport At Rest Client.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Transport At Rest Client assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Transport At Rest Client, keep Transport At Rest Client connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

content.text: A review Go file-delivery team handles case 7170 during a regional dependency incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate transport, storage, KMS authorization, context, and rotation evidence; record cleanup, rollback, restoration, and residual-risk ownership; then double concurrent transfers and keep process resource limits intact. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate TLS in transit, S3 server-side encryption, KMS envelope encryption, and application client-side encryption by threat and feature tradeoff.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Transport At Rest Client has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

Competency probe: Separate TLS in transit, S3 server-side encryption, KMS envelope encryption, and application client-side encryption by threat and feature tradeoff.

For S3, CloudFront, and Go Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Transport At Rest Client, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: SSE-KMS keeps plaintext hidden from the application and S3 service.

options.text: Declare S3, CloudFront, and Go stakeholder outcome, file and object identity, owner, state, and byte, time, concurrency, cache, and cost budgets for the review Transport At Rest Client case
Predict S3, CloudFront, and Go authorization, custody, transfer, integrity, cache, failure, cancellation, cleanup, rollback, and recovery outcomes for the review Transport At Rest Client case
Run the smallest deterministic S3, CloudFront, and Go storage decision model and retain inspectable changed-case evidence for the review Transport At Rest Client case

lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Prove new writes, old reads, role rotation, key disable recovery, compromised-session revocation limits, and residual cached or signed access. Artifact under review: encryption and key-authorization contract.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Key Rotation Revocation Drill, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Key Rotation Revocation Drill record says encryption and key-authorization contract looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Key Rotation Revocation Drill.

For separate evidence from confidence, run one convenient Key Rotation Revocation Drill happy path, omit ownership, error, changed input, goroutine stop, and full-toolchain checks, then accept plausible output. This does not satisfy Key Rotation Revocation Drill for encryption and key-authorization contract.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Key Rotation Revocation Drill
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Key Rotation Revocation Drill: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review Go file-delivery team handles case 8216 during a cost-anomaly investigation. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate transport, storage, KMS authorization, context, and rotation evidence; preserve accessible progress, cancellation, status, and recovery evidence; then introduce a duplicate event or ambiguous response and reconcile without duplicate effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Prove new writes, old reads, role rotation, key disable recovery, compromised-session revocation limits, and residual cached or signed access.

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Key Rotation Revocation Drill.
steps.why: Retrieving Key Rotation Revocation Drill through the concrete encryption and key-authorization contract case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Key Rotation Revocation Drill, keep Key Rotation Revocation Drill connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Prove new writes, old reads, role rotation, key disable recovery, compromised-session revocation limits, and residual cached or signed access.

At S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Key Rotation Revocation Drill, trace this concrete input and predict runtime validation, authorization, custody, integrity, range, stream, cache, retry, cost, cleanup, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A review Go file-delivery team handles case 8216 during a cost-anomaly investigation. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate transport, storage, KMS authorization, context, and rotation evidence; preserve accessible progress, cancellation, status, and recovery evidence; then introduce a duplicate event or ambiguous response and reconcile without duplicate effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Prove new writes, old reads, role rotation, key disable recovery, compromised-session revocation limits, and residual cached or signed access.

For S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Key Rotation Revocation Drill, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: Rotating a KMS key immediately re-encrypts every existing object and revokes every URL.
